clusters.title: Roll up, roll up! Older generation find joy and fearlessness in circus
clusters.neutral_headline: Older Generation Finds Joy In Circus Activities

items.title: Roll up, roll up! Older generation find joy and fearlessness in circus
items.standfirst: Juggling, trapeze and hula hoop classes for over-50s are taking off as people rediscover their inner child and tap into health benefitsRumman Talukder’s favourite circus trick is called the Mermaid. Every Sunday, the 60-year-old IT consultant drives from his home in Stanmore to a circus school in Ware to practise it. Hanging from a trapeze by one arm, with his back arched and his legs wrapped around the rope, he says it makes him feel “strong and graceful”.“My wife thinks I’m mad but in the run-up to turning 60, I decided I wanted to challenge myself; to find things not normally associated with people my age,” he says. Continue reading...
items.content: Juggling, trapeze and hula hoop classes for over-50s are taking off as people rediscover their inner child and tap into health benefitsRumman Talukder’s favourite circus trick is called the Mermaid. Every Sunday, the 60-year-old IT consultant drives from his home in Stanmore to a circus school in Ware to practise it. Hanging from a trapeze by one arm, with his back arched and his legs wrapped around the rope, he says it makes him feel “strong and graceful”.“My wife thinks I’m mad but in the run-up to turning 60, I decided I wanted to challenge myself; to find things not normally associated with people my age,” he says. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Circus classes are available for people over 50 years old
Activities include juggling and trapeze lessons
Participants report feeling strong and graceful
The classes help people rediscover their inner child
The activities have physical and mental health benefits

items.title: ‘A genuine wildlife emergency’: everything you need to know about the arrival of H5 bird flu in Australia
items.standfirst: What is the virus? Where did it come from? Are humans at risk? What does it mean for the agricultural sector and wildlife? Experts answer your questionsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA deadly strain of bird flu, known as H5 bird flu, has arrived on the Australian mainland.The federal agriculture minister, Julie Collins, confirmed in late June that a brown skua – found sick in Western Australia – had died from the H5N1 virus. The skua is a wild migratory bird. Continue reading...

The skua is a wild migratory bird. Continue reading...

items.title: NHS to use AI on its app to direct patients to appropriate services
items.standfirst: Update in England expected to reach about 200,000 patients over the next year as part of £10bn package to overhaul NHS systemsThe NHS will begin using AI on its app to direct patients to the appropriate services, it has been announced.The tool will be used to triage patients and to ascertain if they should be allocated a GP appointment. Some may be advised to attend a pharmacy or their local A&E department instead, depending on the severity of their condition. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Consider the parents caring for adult children not in work or education | Letter
clusters.neutral_headline: Consider the parents caring for adult children not in work or education | Letter

items.title: Consider the parents caring for adult children not in work or education | Letter
items.standfirst: One reader was surprised by how many people responding to Annalisa Barbieri’s advice column equate successful parenting with independenceReading the comments below Annalisa Barbieri’s latest advice column online (I wish my son wanted to spend more time with me, 28 June), I was struck by how many people equated successful parenting with raising independent adult children.But life isn’t always that straightforward. There is a largely invisible generation of midlife parents still caring for adult children who remain Neets – not in employment, education or training. Many are living with neurodivergence, severe mental illness, post-Covid syndrome or chronic ill health. Continue reading...
items.content: One reader was surprised by how many people responding to Annalisa Barbieri’s advice column equate successful parenting with independenceReading the comments below Annalisa Barbieri’s latest advice column online (I wish my son wanted to spend more time with me, 28 June), I was struck by how many people equated successful parenting with raising independent adult children.But life isn’t always that straightforward. There is a largely invisible generation of midlife parents still caring for adult children who remain Neets – not in employment, education or training. Many are living with neurodivergence, severe mental illness, post-Covid syndrome or chronic ill health. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Many adult children are not in work or education

clusters.title: Deaths in France surged 30% during hottest week of record June heatwave
clusters.neutral_headline: France Sees Surge In Deaths During Heatwave

items.title: Deaths in France surged 30% during hottest week of record June heatwave
items.standfirst: Public health authority says 2,025 excess deaths probably an underestimate and that it expects toll to rise furtherThe number of deaths recorded in France surged by nearly 30% during the hottest week of the record-breaking heatwave that scorched much of Europe last month, the public health authority has said, adding that it expected the toll to rise further.Public Health France said on Friday there had been “an increase of 29.1%, corresponding to 2,025 additional deaths compared with the previous week”. It said the figure was probably an underestimate and “mortality will rise further”. Continue reading...
items.content: Public health authority says 2,025 excess deaths probably an underestimate and that it expects toll to rise furtherThe number of deaths recorded in France surged by nearly 30% during the hottest week of the record-breaking heatwave that scorched much of Europe last month, the public health authority has said, adding that it expected the toll to rise further.Public Health France said on Friday there had been “an increase of 29.1%, corresponding to 2,025 additional deaths compared with the previous week”. It said the figure was probably an underestimate and “mortality will rise further”. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Deaths in France increased by nearly 30 percent
Heatwave causes 2025 excess deaths in one week
Public Health France reports excess deaths
Mortality rate expected to rise further
Heatwave affects public health in France
